comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Verified in current checkout. extensions/tau/src/agents-menu/state.ts:138-152 implements a local temp-write + rename helper (writeSettingsFile) using fs.writeFile + fs.rename + best-effort temp cleanup, with no fsync of temp or final file. The repo already has a shared helper in extensions/tau/src/shared/atomic-write.ts providing atomicWriteFileString/atomicWriteFileStringSync with temp-file creation, rename, and fsync on temp and final file handles. So this is real duplication and bypasses the stronger shared durability path. This is a maintainability/durability issue more than an immediate correctness failure, but the review claim is directionally valid.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Follow-up verification on broader 'atomic writes are still duplicated' claim: partially valid. agents-menu/state.ts still has a bespoke temp+rename writer, so shared atomic-write is not yet the sole implementation. However, the cited sync writes in extensions/tau/src/autoresearch/index.ts at lines 1172 and 1316 already use atomicWriteFileStringSync from shared/atomic-write.ts, so that part of the review is outdated for current HEAD. Keep this issue focused on eliminating remaining local writer variants and standardizing on shared atomic-write helpers.
